Danny: My name is Danny Perasa. This is my wife, Annie. We are married for over sixty years.

Annie: Sixty-three years.

Danny: Yes, sixty-three years. Every day, I leave Annie a love note. I write it on paper. I put it where she will find it. Sometimes in her purse. Sometimes on the kitchen table. Sometimes on her pillow.

Annie: He does this every day. I never know where I will find it. But I always do.

Danny: I write simple things. "I love you." "You make me happy." "You are beautiful." Sometimes I write longer notes. I write about our life. I write about our memories.

Annie: When I find the note, I read it. I smile. It makes my day. It reminds me he still loves me. After all these years, he still cares.

Danny: I do it because I want her to know. I want her to know every day. She is loved. She is important to me. Life is short. I do not want to waste a day. I tell her how I feel.

Annie: One day, I asked him, "Danny, when you leave me, how will I live? How will I live without your notes?"

Danny: I told her, "Annie, I wrote enough notes. I wrote enough for your whole life. You will find them. I hid them everywhere. In books, in drawers, in your favorite places. You will find them when you need them most."

Annie: He is right. I find notes in strange places. In old books, in my jewelry box, even in my coat pocket from years ago. He is preparing for this. He writes notes for my future, for when he is gone.

Danny: I love her. I loved her for sixty-three years. I will love her forever. These notes are my way to be with her. Even when I cannot be there.

Annie: We have a beautiful life together. We raised children. We traveled. We laughed. We cried. But through it all, there is one constant: his love notes. They remind me love does not end. It continues, even after someone is gone.

Danny: That is what I want her to remember. That is what I want her to know. Love is forever. We will always be a family, no matter what happens.

Annie: We will always be a family. Always.
